task_name: Extract Service Layer from Route Handlers

plan_metrics.plan_content:
Here's the implementation plan:

---

## Plan: Extract Service Layer

### New files (3)

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `src/services/service-error.ts` | `ServiceError(statusCode, message)` class â€” thrown by services, caught in routes |
| `src/services/product-service.ts` | `listProducts`, `getProduct`, `adjustStock` |
| `src/services/order-service.ts` | `listOrders`, `getOrder`, `createOrder`, `updateOrderStatus` |

### Modified files (2)

- **`src/routes/products.ts`** â€” stripped to HTTP parsing + service calls + error mapping
- **`src/routes/orders.ts`** â€” same treatment

### Unchanged (5)
`src/app.ts`, `src/types.ts`, `src/data.ts`, both test files â€” no changes needed.

---

### Error strategy
Services **throw `ServiceError`**. Routes wrap service calls in `try/catch` and map `ServiceError` â†’ `res.status(e.statusCode).json({ error: e.message })`. This keeps route handlers as straight-line code with a single catch block.

### HTTP vs. business logic boundary

| Stays in routes (HTTP) | Moves to services (business) |
|---|---|
| `parseInt` / `parseFloat` query params | Filtering arrays by category, price, userId, status |
| `typeof adjustment !== "number"` body guard | Computing `isLowStock`, `inventoryValue`, summaries |
| `!Array.isArray(input.items)` presence check | Enriching order items with `productName` |
| Calling `res.json()` / `res.status()` | Order validation, stock deduction, status transitions |

### Verification
```bash
npm test   # all 13 existing vitest/supertest tests must pass
```
